codeReview.summary: Clean, well-executed refactoring that consolidates 4 separate getSupabaseClient() implementations into a single lazy-cached singleton in web/lib/supabase.ts and replaces all plain <a> tags with Next.js <Link> components in the sidebar. The diff shows 41 insertions vs 84 deletions — net code reduction. All 4 consumer files (dashboard-client.tsx, todo-board.tsx, todo-actions.ts, logs/page.tsx) now import from the shared module. Only web/lib/supabase.ts imports from @supabase/supabase-js. No remaining <a href=> patterns. The singleton pattern uses a module-level cachedClient variable with null-check, which is the standard approach for client-side Supabase usage. The old unused `export const supabase` (never imported anywhere) was correctly removed. TypeScript compilation, Next.js build, and all 317 agent tests pass.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md does not document that all web code should import getSupabaseClient from '@/lib/supabase' rather than creating local Supabase clients. This was the root cause of the original duplication (4 files each had their own implementation). Adding a convention like 'Supabase client: always use getSupabaseClient() from web/lib/supabase.ts — never import createClient directly in component files' would prevent recurrence.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Prior to this fix, 4 files each had independent getSupabaseClient() implementations. AGENTS.md mentions Supabase but only says 'publishable key for web' — no guidance on the import pattern.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature references skillName 'frontend-worker' but no .factory/skills/frontend-worker/SKILL.md exists. Cannot evaluate whether the worker followed the skill procedure since the skill file is missing entirely.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json has skillName: 'frontend-worker'. Glob for .factory/skills/**/SKILL.md returns no results. .factory/ directory has no skills/ subdirectory.

summary: PASS. The fix adequately addresses all 3 non-blocking issues from round 1: (1) getSupabaseClient() now uses a lazy-cached singleton pattern instead of creating a new client on every call, (2) all 4 duplicate Supabase client patterns are consolidated into a single shared module (web/lib/supabase.ts), and (3) all 4 plain <a> tags in the sidebar navigation are replaced with Next.js <Link> components for client-side routing. The implementation is clean, reduces code by 43 net lines, and all verification steps pass (tsc, next build, vitest). No blocking or non-blocking issues found.
